title: Trek 8700
description:
Composite frame, color is Black/gray with indigo coloring on back (wheel) frame.  I have a "Gincleey" comfort bike seat on it but it has several strips of black Gorilla tape on it (because there were some tears in the seat). 

Bike purchased <1 week ago (4/25/26) from WashCo Bikes - I never looked at the serial number, and I called WashCo Bikes to see if they had this info, but they said they do not keep record of this.  They DID however take my first and last name (and I believe contact info) when I purchased the bike.  

Also! The bike shop did add a sturdy kickstand!

title: 2000 Dahon Bike Radar or Jack
description: Photo is stock photo. Our Bike is circa 2000 discontinued tall frame by Dahon - Dahon is written in white on our bike. It also has a faded yellow and worn/ peeling sticker that reads "TO TOKYO"  and "TEAM relocations" from when it was shipped by us there in 2006. The back mudguard is a "quick/easy release" mudguard over the back wheel. Original seat is worn. Fair amount of knicks and scratches on the bike. Stolen in MidCity.

title: 2007 Dahon
description:
A Dahon Matrix full-size folding bicycle with many modifications since it was purchased new in 2007. Front tire replaced with 26x1.95 Crossroads Armadillo Reflect. Rear tire replaced with 26x1.95 Hemisphere Armadillo Reflect. Front and rear full-length fenders installed.

The only photograph I have is this top view shot of my son playing with the pedals. One can find a decent photo of the bicycle by searching for Dahon Matrix 2007 on the internet (https://live.staticflickr.com/1094/824117577_1085f28e15_k.jpg is the best I've found), but these photos will not show the wheels and fenders that are on my bicycle.

payload.description:
A Dahon Matrix full-size folding bicycle with many modifications since it was purchased new in 2007. Front tire replaced with 26x1.95 Crossroads Armadillo Reflect. Rear tire replaced with 26x1.95 Hemisphere Armadillo Reflect. Front and rear full-length fenders installed.

The only photograph I have is this top view shot of my son playing with the pedals. One can find a decent photo of the bicycle by searching for Dahon Matrix 2007 on the internet (https://live.staticflickr.com/1094/824117577_1085f28e15_k.jpg is the best I've found), but these photos will not show the wheels and fenders that are on my bicycle.

title: 2011 Dahon Vitesse
description: s fold bike is very distinct from the other Dahon Vitesse models: In order to increase visibility, I covered the entire frame with 3M REFLECTIVE VINYL TAPE, so at NIGHT the bike will GLOW like a stop sign. During the day, the bike looks black, as if it were covered with black electrical tape. Yet with any little hint of light reflecting off of it, it will GLOW. The CARGO RACK REMOVED The bike CHAIN PROTECTOR REMOVED The CAP at the bottom of the BIKE POST that houses the inner tire pump REMOVED. The serial number is etched at the bottom of the bike where the crank set is installed which reads DU07705083

title: 2013 Surly Moonlander
description:
Upgraded 2013 Surly Moonlander (Large, 20" seat tube). Dark (Space?) black/gray metallic paint (white decals), some rust at headset, mounts and bottom bracket. Offset fork (slight "S' bend) due to narrow wheel hubs. Front hub has additional threading on one side. Original Clown Shoe rims w black rim liner. Bontrager Barbegazi (?) tires 27.5 x 4.5" (prob have red iron dust on them). Upgraded gruppo: single front ring (orig was double ring), massive rear 11 or 12 speed cassette with "stump puller" type cog. Sram Level T hydraulic disc brakes (o.g. were cable actuated Shimano). Ergo grips on flat mountain style handlebar. 45North pedals, black with orange spikes/teeth (some worn and broken off). orig WTB saddle.

Posted photos are prior to upgrades.

Surly Moonlander, Surly 4130 CroMoly steel. Main triangle double-butted. TIG-welded

➋ Color: silver with black and yellow detail and blue / aqua handlebar wrap

title: 2021 Santa Cruz Blur Carbon S
description: Black with orange decals on frame and SID fork.  I REMOVED THE DROPPER POST.  It is a black carbon Whiskey Parts Co. seatpost.  2 water bottle cages meant to be removed by a left-handed rider, not right-handed.  Selle Italia SLR Boost Superflow Saddle with Ti rails.  SRAM GX cabled components, not AXS.  Deore XTR pedals.  Lezyne tire plug tool is the handlebar plug in either the left or right side, I can't remember which.  Maxxis Aspen tires, 29" x 2.25".  Rear tire needs more sealant or to be replaced as it loses air quickly.

title: 2022 Specialized Levo Carbon Comp
description:
PRODUCT SPECIFICATIONS
Battery

Specialized M3-700, Integrated Battery, 700wh

Cassette

Sram Xg-1275, 12-Speed, 10-52t

Chain

Sram Nx Eagle, 12-Speed

Chainrings

Sram X-Sync Eagle, 104 Bcd, 32t, Steel

Charger

Custom Charger, 42v4a W/ Rosenberger Plug, 100-240v

Crankset

Praxis Forged M30, Custom Offset, 160mm

Fork

Fox Float 36 Rhythm 29, Grip Damper, 44mm Offset, 2-Position Sweep Adjust, 15x110mm, 1.5" Tapered Steerer, 160mm Travel

Frame

Fact 11m Full Carbon, 29" Front Wheel, 27.5" Rear Wheel, Full Internal Cable Routing, 148mm Spacing, Fully Sealed Cartridge Bearings, 150mm Of Travel, Geo Adjust Head Tube, Geo Adjust Horst Pivot

Front Brake

Sram Code Rs, 4-Piston Caliper, Hydraulic Disc, S1-S3: 200mm, S4-S6: 220mm

Front Hub

Alloy, Sealed Cartridge Bearings, 15x110mm Thru-Axle, 28h

Front Tire

Butcher, Grid Trail Casing, Gripton® T9 Compound, 2bliss Ready, 29x2.6"

Handlebars

Specialized, 6061 Alloy, 6-Degree Upsweep, 8-Degree Backsweep, 30mm Rise, 780mm Width

Inner Tubes

Standard, Presta Valve

Motor

Specialized Turbo Full Power System 2.2 Motor

Rear Brake

Sram Code Rsc, 4-Piston Caliper, Hydraulic Disc, 200mm

Rear Derailleur

Sram Gx Eagle, 12-Speed

Rear Hub

Alloy, Sealed Cartridge Bearings, 148mmx12mm Thru-Axle, 28h

Rear Shock

S1: Fox Dps Performance Series, 3-Position Lever, Rebound Adjust, 52.5 X 210mm, S2-S6: Fox Float X Performance, Lsr, 2-Position Lever, 55x210mm

Rear Tire

Eliminator, Grid Trail Casing, Gripton® T7 Compound, 2bliss Ready, 27.5x2.6"

Rims

Specialized 29, Hookless Alloy, 30mm Inner Width, Tubeless Ready

Saddle

Bridge Comp, Hollow Cr-Mo Rails, 155/143mm

Seat Binder

Alloy, 38.6

Seatpost

X-Fusion Manic, Infinite Adjustable, Two-Bolt Head, Bottom Mount Cable Routing, Remote Slr Le Lever, 34.9, S1: 100mm, S2: 125mm, S3: 150mm, S4/S5: 175mm, S6: 190mm

Shift Levers

Sram Gx Eagle, 12-Speed

Spokes

Dt Swiss Industry

Stem

Alloy Trail Stem, 35mm Bar Bore

Swat

Swat Cc Steerer Tube Integrated Tool With Chain Tool And Link

Tape

Specialized Trail Grips

Ui/Remote

Specialized Mastermind Tcu, Percentage Of Remaining Charge, 120 Possible Display Configurations, Microtune Assist Adjustment, Over-The-Air Updates, Ant+/Bluetooth®, W/Handlebar Remote

Wiring Harness

Custom Specialized Wiring Harness

title: 2023 Fuji Nevada Medium
description:
Fuji Nevada hardtail with upgraded after market shifter/crank and gears. The primary color is dark turquoise with black Fuji decals. Accent color of a neon light green pedals and bottle cage with yellow neon handle grips. There's also a sticker on the frame that says "No Stupid People Beyond This Point" and a Jerks Bike shop sticker. This is where my son bought his bike. This Mountain bike is priceless to my son. He saved and earned every dime for this bike at the age of 14.5 years old. He is on Murray High School Mountain Bike team and cannot participate without it. I'm a single mom trying to do my best on a poverty income doing what I can to climb out of poverty while just surviving paying bills. My son is heart broken that someone took not just his bike but his ability to be on his team which he also paid for and recieved scholarship for the year. 
As a single mom, I rely on him being able to take himself to and from school on his bike. It's so cruel that someone stole it. The school cameras have the footage of the thief. Unfortunately the thief has a hoodie on and his face covered.

title: 2020 REI Co-op DRT 2.1 Mountain Bike
description: The bike is a Co-op Cycles DRT 2.1 mountain bike, size XL, with a 6061 aluminum hardtail frame in the stock REI color scheme. It has a Suntour SR AIR-Boost front suspension fork with 140 mm travel, a SRAM SX Eagle 1×12 drivetrain with an 11–50T cassette, and Tektro Auriga hydraulic disc brakes front and rear. The wheels are 27.5-inch with 2.8-inch wide tires, tubeless-ready rims, upgraded pedals to black Chester pedals . It is equipped with an X-Fusion Manic dropper seatpost with a handlebar-mounted remote. The bike also features a WTB Volt Sport saddle, riser handlebars, and weighs about 34 pounds in this XL frame size.

title: 2021 Diamondback Powerline - Hardtail
description: Have you wanted to experience the thrill of mountain biking but are not thrilled with the idea of having to do all the work up the climbs? Look no further than the Diamondback Powerline E-Bike. With 5 different levels of pedal assist, plus a walk mode, you can choose when and how much power assistance you receive. A Shimano drivetrain provides reliable gear changes throughout the 10 speeds, a 100mm fork provides front suspension to smooth out the rough trail, and hydraulic disc brakes give more powerful and consistent braking performance; especially in variable conditions for those wet and muddy days. Whether you’re looking for a faster & easier ride into work, exploring non-paved trails or venturing up (and eventually down) the mountain trails, the Diamondback Powerline Mountain Bike with Electric Pedal Assist will have you enjoying a new sense of freedom and fun.

title: 2020 Mafia Lucky 6 STB
description:
Bike was stolen in Deptford, whilst I was riding it. Crime Reference 01/7685540/25

Mafia Lucky 6: Frame is a two tone blue, possibly 2020 season. Muck Off Sticker on underside of frame, where pale blue meets darker blue. Scratches on dark blue RHS of frame; Horizon V2 Carbon Riser MTB Handlebar 31.8mm; Collective Headset (Black); Hope top cap & bolt in black with blue Hope spacers; Shimano Deore derailer & shifter; Clarks brake (black). Brake calliper snapped leaving one bolt holding it together; & Blue & white suede Collective LH seat.

Other bike in photos: My friend also had his bike stolen at the same time. He had a Black & Purple C100, Purple Wake Stem; Nukeproof handle bars. Rockshock Judys suspension, rear Chosen Hub, black Selects Cranks; purple chain ring; PC Specialist sticker on downtube of seat post; Collective sticker on downtube on seat post.

title: Falcon (frame) San Remo
description:
Black Brooks leather saddle, lugged 1" steel fork (slightly different shade of blue), Handlebar - Nitto Albatross, 55cm x 25.4 × 1.
There is a rack mounted to the fork cantilever mounts and a black Wald basket sitting atop the rack, with extra Wald supports attached to eyelets located lower on the fork blades.
Rear hub is a Shimano Saint (MTB) with a through axle, which threads into the derailleur.  This is a unique feature of this bike - there is no derailleur hangar.     

The frame has a unique junction of the seat stays, which curve over the top of the seat & down tubes to meet one another.

title: Supercycle BREEZE
description:
Color: Dark blue frame with a glossy finish. The paint shows minor scuffs and wear, particularly near the bottom bracket and chainstay, reflecting typical usage.

Frame Size & Design: The bike features a medium-sized frame with a slightly slanted top tube. The tube joints are visibly welded and reinforced, providing extra durability for off-road or urban riding.

Gears & Drivetrain: Equipped with a standard multi-speed gear setup. It has a triple chainring in the front and a 7-speed cassette in the rear, allowing for smooth shifting over a range of terrains. The derailleurs and shifters are operational but show minor signs of use.

Brakes: The bike uses traditional rim brakes, both front and rear, which are in good working order. The brake levers on the handlebars are black and slightly worn from use.

Handlebars: The handlebars are flat, straight, and black, with wear visible on the grips. The right grip end has a small cap missing, exposing the inner handlebar tube. The handlebar stem shows slight rust and wear, while the cables for the brakes and shifters are externally routed, curving smoothly toward the frame.

Tires & Wheels: The tires are thick, knobby, and designed for multi-surface riding, with a deep tread pattern ideal for gravel or rough urban surfaces. The tires are mounted on aluminum rims, which appear to be in good shape. Both wheels are secured with standard quick-release mechanisms.

Saddle: The saddle is a basic black vinyl model, showing slight wear but no visible damage. It's supported by a standard seat post with a quick-release mechanism for easy height adjustments.

Water Bottle & Holder: White water bottle that says 'Silvera for Seniors' is a sipper style 500 ml bottle and is on a metal bottle cage that is located in the vertical rod of the bike just above the front gear chain

Accessories:

Lock: A black coiled cable lock is attached around the frame, securing the bike when parked.
Green Bag: A small green bag is mounted on the handlebar stem, possibly used for small items or tools.
Reflectors: There are red rear reflectors and front-mounted white reflectors for visibility.
Bar Code: The bike features a barcode sticker on the frame near the handlebar with the number "70410 01847."

Distinctive Marks: The handlebar stem has visible rust and slight wear, while the grips show signs of extended use.

Saddle 	Specialized Phenom 143mm width
Front Wheel 	Elitewheels Carbon SLR-MTB, Asymetric, 36mm width x 24mm depth, 12k gloss weave,  110x15mm axle

title: 2023 Sirrus Specialized Sirrus X 2.0
description:
Bicycle Description
	•	Brand/Model: Specialized Sirrus X 2.0
	•	Type: Hybrid / fitness commuter bike
	•	Frame: Aluminum, step-over (diamond frame)
	•	Color: Deep burgundy / maroon (matte finish)
	•	Wheels/Tires: 700c wheels with wider, semi-slick tires (designed for pavement + light gravel)
	•	Drivetrain: 1x drivetrain (single front chainring with multiple rear gears)
	•	Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes
	•	Handlebars: Flat handlebars (upright riding position)
	•	Fork: Rigid (no suspension)

Accessories/Identifiers:
	•	Kickstand installed
	•	Front and rear reflectors
	•	multi color chevron Saddle bag (at time of theft, if applicable)
	•	Distinct dark maroon frame color with minimal branding

-Rims: Enve XC, with 45mm schwalbe tires and fenders
-Blue growtec equal brakes
-Dark brown brooks saddle 
-Carbon fork with black widefoot cargo mounts
-ZIPP handle bars, blue bar tape
-black cargo rack with 2 black ortlieb panniers (attached when stolen)
-SRAM GX derailleur, SRAM apex crackset
-bikepump+water bottle cage attached to water bottle space
-Sticker on upper bar that says "Carinito"
payload.title: 2014 Salsa Vaya

title: GT Bicycles Transeo 4.0
description:
The GT Transeo 4.0 is a hybrid bike designed for riders who want something versatile — comfortable for commuting and fitness rides, but also capable of handling light trails and gravel. Here are the main details about it:
Overview
Category: Hybrid / Fitness / Commuter
Brand: GT Bicycles 
Frame: Aluminum (lightweight, durable, upright geometry)
Fork: Equipped with a front suspension fork with a lockout option for efficient road riding
Wheels: 700c wheels (like a road bike, but wider tires for stability and comfort)
Speeds/Drivetrain: Typically a 3x8 (24-speed) Shimano or microSHIFT drivetrain
Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes (reliable stopping power in all weather, but not hydraulic)
Tires: 700x40c (good balance of speed and shock absorption)
Other features: Rack and fender mounts, blue MEC carrying panniers.

title: Kona
description:
Bike was stolen from the back of my parked car in the PSU Blumel Hall garage. Noticed stolen at 14:20 on Wednesday, June 25, 2025.

Frame & Fork:

- Frame: ICAN X-Gravel (54cm / Medium)
- Fork: ICAN X-Gravel (included)
- Purchase: January 2023 ($860 incl. shipping)

Drivetrain:

- Crankset: FSA SL-K Team Issue 386evo (172.5mm)
- Chainring: Raceface 38t Narrow-Wide
- Cassette: SRAM XX1 11-speed
- Shifter/Derailleur: Shimano SLX
- Bottom Bracket: FSA

Wheels & Tires:

- Rims: WTB i19
- Hubs:
    - Front: Shimano 105
    - Rear: Stans 3.30
- Spokes: Sapim Laser
- Tires: Maxxis DTR-1 (650b x 47mm)

Brakes:

- Brakes: Shimano Metrea (flat mount)
- Rotors: Shimano XTR (140mm front/rear)

Cockpit:

- Handlebars: Renthal Fatbar Carbon Lite (690mm, 20mm rise, 31.8mm clamp)
- Stem: Kona (60mm)
- Grips: ODI Vapor

Seating:

- Seatpost: FSA XC-190 (27.2mm diameter)
- Saddle: WTB Volt (Kona-branded)

Extras:

- Fenders: Planet Bike Cascadia
- Pedals: Kona JS2 pedals

	•	Brand/Model: Specialized Sirrus X 2.0

	•	Color: Deep burgundy / maroon (matte finish)

	•	Fork: Rigid (no suspension)

Accessories/Identifiers:

	•	Distinct dark maroon frame color with minimal branding

title: GT Bicycles Transeo 4.0
description:
The GT Transeo 4.0 is a hybrid bike designed for riders who want something versatile — comfortable for commuting and fitness rides, but also capable of handling light trails and gravel. Here are the main details about it:
Overview
Category: Hybrid / Fitness / Commuter
Brand: GT Bicycles 
Frame: Aluminum (lightweight, durable, upright geometry)
Fork: Equipped with a front suspension fork with a lockout option for efficient road riding
Wheels: 700c wheels (like a road bike, but wider tires for stability and comfort)
Speeds/Drivetrain: Typically a 3x8 (24-speed) Shimano or microSHIFT drivetrain
Brakes: Mechanical disc brakes (reliable stopping power in all weather, but not hydraulic)
Tires: 700x40c (good balance of speed and shock absorption)
Other features: Rack and fender mounts, blue MEC carrying panniers.

title: Concord 22MB2702YD
description: Concord 27.5" Commuter Step-Over Electric Bike. This electric bike is equipped with a reliable 350W high-speed brushless rear hub motor, providing a torque curve that seamlessly augments your pedaling power. The 36V 10Ah battery, which is portable and lockable, offers a range of up to 20 miles per charge. The user-friendly display provides real-time information such as pedal assist level, power status, speed, trip distance, and headlight switch control. The gear level operates smoothly, mimicking a traditional bicycle without resistance. Additionally, the Shimano 7-speed gear with trigger shifters and derailleur ensures quick, precise gear shifts, enabling you to navigate any terrain with remarkable ease.

title: 1994 Specialized Crossroads
description:
I came outside this morning 12/7/24 to find my prized commuter had disappeared from my backyard. Near City Park and Mountain. Likely stolen in the night. It was a barn find that I rebuilt and added many non-stock parts parts to, and I haven't seen seen anything similar to it around town. Unfortunately I did not write down the serial number, but I will be filing reports with Bike Index and the city.

~1994 Red Specialized Crossroads in XL/Large with White Decals

UPANBIKE butterfly bars Wrapped in Red (Most Distinctive)
Black Avid Speed Dial Brake Levers
Koski V-Brake front/ Avid Single Digit Rear Calipers
No-name 3x8 Speed Shifters
Shimano Deore LX Rear Derailleur
Vintage Shimano Exage? Front Derailleur
Puroma Red Pedals
Black Easton Front Wheel - Specialized Sawtooth Tire
Dura-Ace Hub With Mavic Rim Rear Wheel - Goodyear Transit Speed Tire
Silver Sunrace 11-40t Cassette on the Rear Wheel (Big Gear on back wheel)
Bontrager Seat

Other Stuff:

Faded Brown REI Flash Pannier on left side
Jim Blackburn Bottle Cage and JBlackburn Rear Rack
Adjustable Bottle Cage on seat tube
RockBros Tubetop Bag and Frame Bag
Polar Colorado Water Bottle
Red Phone Mount
Blitzu Bike Light (Paint Heavily Faded)
OnGuard U Lock (Unlocked)

title: 2023 Aventón Step-through
description:
Level.2 Step-Through Commuter Ebike, the color is white (officially called "polar"). It has a black metal basket on the back, and a red bell and a phone holder in the front (but who knows what the thieves might detach). The display is scratched and covered with transparent tape. The bike has about 3,000 miles and I've been driving it for >220 hours (according to the display as far as I remember). I added reflectors to the spokes.

The bike trailer (also stolen along with the bike) is Burley Honey Bee and can also be turned into a stroller. It's red, also has reflectors on the spikes, and the tires are Schwalbe Marathon Plus. Inside in the front, the bottom was ripped and fixed with black roof tape from the outside bottom, with a little whole down in the middle.

frame_model: moped-style, retro design with a short wheelbase and 20-inch fat tires, built from an aluminum alloy for a blend of lightness and strength, featuring a distinctive banana seat and integrated lights, making it both functional and stylish. It's essentially a classic, vintage scooter look adapted into a modern, powerful electric bicycle. Key Frame Characteristics: Design: Moped-style, retro, often compared to classic mopeds or mini-bikes. Material: Aluminum alloy, sometimes combined with steel for durability. Tires: 20-inch fat tires for stability on varied terrains. Seat: Texas-style banana seat with detailed stitching for comfort and style. Components: Includes front suspension, integrated LED lights, turn signals, and a unique overall look with tidy cable management. In essence, the frame model is a stylish, durable, retro-inspired design built for comfort and versatility, common to the OUXI V8 series.

title: 2024 Lectric Cycles XPedition
description:
Our white Lectric XPedition cargo ebike was stolen on 11/17/2025 at 1:51pm while parked outside of Alcott Elementary in Bay Ho (4680 Hidalgo Ave, San Diego, CA 92117). The bike was parked outside while a parent was inside briefly, so we suspect it was a crime of opportunity by someone who lives nearby. The bike has dual batteries, a rear cargo rack (for kids to sit in), rear passenger padding, running boards, and a Kryptonite chain lock which was locked around the rear rack. They won’t have the charger. The thief was wearing a light grey hoodie and I believe plaid pajama pants when they stole the bike. Thief may be female. 

We used this bike to take our child to school and would love to get it back. $500 reward for return of the bike. Please call or text with any info: 734-717-9194.

title: Ariel Rider X-Class 52 V
description:
Blue Ariel Rider 52V bike with black seat and black passenger seat. 

The bike will have scratches at the front of the bike near the post due to the chain lock that I usually tie around there while not in use. 

Will have a black and red bike bell on the right side of the handlebars. 

Has HEB allscape tires mounted on them. The rear tire is mounted backwards so will have Rotation arrow pointing wrong direction. 

Unfortunately do not have the serial number for this bike written down. 

The battery was taken with the bike and is locked to the frame. I still have the key to unlock the battery and can bring that in to prove the bike is mine. Serial number of battery is located on the back where it meets the bike frame
